rulings.Case Title: UNIVERSAL INTERACTIVE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al VS. CONTINENTAL CASUALTY COMPANY, AN ILLINOIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Universal Interactive, Llc, Joel Shamitoff And John Lucero'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 30, 2011, line 15, Plaintiffs Universal Interactive, Llc, Joel Shamitoff And John Lucero's Motion For Summary Adjudication. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. THE DUTY TO DEFEND IS IMMEDIATE AND LASTS UNTIL THE LAWSUIT IS OVER OR THERE IS A SHOWING THAT THERE IS NO POTENTIAL FOR COVERAGE. SEE MONTROSE V. S.C. (1993) 6 CAL.4TH 287; CENTURY SURETY CO. V. POLISSO (2006) 139 CAL.APP.4TH 922.) DEFENDANT'S DENIAL LETTER IS BASED SOLELY ON 1 d (PRIOR LITIGATION EXCLUSION) STATING THE WILLIAMS AND RED CAP ACTIONS INVOLVED INTERRELATED WRONGFUL ACTS, THE FILING OF THE WILLIAMS COMPLAINT ON SEPTEMBER 1, 2008 PRIOR TO THE INCEPTION OF THE POLICY IS THE CONTROLLING DATE, AND THEREFORE THERE IS NO COVERAGE. HOWEVER, THE WILLIAMS AND THE RED CAP CASES DO NOT HAVE A TIGHT NEXUS. THEY INVOLVED DIFFERENT INVESTORS AND DIFFERENT REPRESENTATIONS MADE AT DIFFERENT TIMES. SEE FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT ADVISOR LLC V. AMERICAN INTERNATIONAL (2002) 506 F.3D 922. AT THE VERY LEAST, THERE WAS A FACTUAL DISPUTE REGARDING WHETHER THE WILLIAMS AND RED CAP ACTIONS WERE INTERRELATED THAT WOULD GIVE RISE TO A DUTY TO DEFEND UNDER MIRPAD, LLC V. CAL INS. GUARANTEE ASSOC. (2005) 132 CAL.APP.4TH 1058. THE COURT OVERRULES THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IRA SERVICES INC. VS. AMERICAN ZURICH INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 30, 2011, line 5, DEFENDANT ZURICH AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS "LEGALLY LIABLE" FOR THE FUNDS IN THE POOLED ACCOUNTS. (SCHUMM DECLARATION PARAGRAPH 16, EX 1-2004 AGREEMENT, PP 25, 27; AMFs 3-6, 9-12, 14-15, 18.) TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED THAT DEFENDANT DELIBERATELY IGNORED THE FACTS PRESENTED BY THE INSURED WHICH DEMONSTRATED COVERAGE, CHANNELED ITS INVESTIGATION TO TRY TO SHOW THAT AN EXCLUSION APPLIED, MISREPRESENTED THE COVERAGE TO THE INSURED, AND REFUSED TO CONTACT OTHER PARTIES, INCLUDING WELLS FARGO, TO ASCERTAIN WHETHER THE PLAINTIFF HELD AND WAS LEGALLY LIABLE FOR THE FUNDS. (AMFs 21-28.) =(302/CWW)
